title: malus × purpurea f. aldenhamensis
intro: Malus × purpurea f. aldenhamensis, also known as Aldenham Crabapple, is a rare cultivar of the Malus purpurea species, belonging to the Rosaceae family and is a hybrid of Malus communis and Malus baccata. It is prized for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent qualities, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Malus × purpurea f. aldenhamensis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Malus × purpurea f. aldenhamensis can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
